title: neltuma tupayachensis
intro: Neltuma tupayachensis, also known as the "Mara" plant, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial infections, resulting in health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced pain, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Neltuma tupayachensis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
